PSP.shortDescription: Neurological syndrome caused by saxitoxins and analogues; block voltage-gated sodium channels. Transmitted via bivalves, crabs, and gastropods.

DSP.shortDescription: Gastrointestinal syndrome caused by okadaic acid and derivatives; inhibit protein phosphatases PP1 and PP2A. Transmitted via bivalves.

ASP.shortDescription: Neurological syndrome caused by domoic acid; excitotoxic glutamate analogue causing hippocampal lesions. Transmitted via bivalves and fish.

NSP.shortDescription: Neurological syndrome caused by brevetoxins; activate voltage-gated sodium channels. Transmitted via bivalves; aerosol irritation also documented.

CFP.shortDescription: Neurological syndrome from ciguatoxins bioaccumulated in coral reef fish; activate sodium channels. Transmitted via herbivorous and piscivorous fish.

AZP.shortDescription: Gastrointestinal and neurotoxic syndrome caused by azaspiracids from Azadinium and related genera. Transmitted via bivalves, particularly mussels.
